essence: Crafts coffee with skill, speed, and hospitality, creating small moments of comfort and connection.

identity.title: Barista
identity.summary: Prepares espresso-based drinks, manages coffee equipment, and provides friendly customer service in cafés and hospitality settings.

salary.notes: Head baristas, specialty coffee professionals, and café managers can exceed £30k–£40k+, with additional earnings in busy or high-end locations.

qualifications: Knowledge of espresso extraction and milk texturing
Understanding of food safety and hygiene standards

lifestyle.description: Baristas work in lively, customer-focused environments where speed, consistency, and friendliness are essential. Days involve pulling espresso shots, steaming milk, preparing drinks, managing queues, cleaning equipment, and creating a welcoming atmosphere. The role can be fast-paced, especially during morning rushes, and often involves standing for long periods. Despite the pace, the work is social, creative, and rewarding — baristas become part of customers’ daily routines and café culture. This lifestyle suits individuals who enjoy hospitality, craft, and working in energetic, community-oriented spaces.

habits: Texturing milk consistently for latte art
